meta.review_note: Deterministic verdicts preserved verbatim (DESIGN §3.1); review adds T3 method-appropriateness and T4 over-reach context and T8 subjective routing that code cannot settle. No deterministic verdict overridden. Computational-chemistry scrutiny: checkable arithmetic claims (delta_hyb, delta_geom, OOH relaxation energies, Table II internal consistency, opposite-sign Cu(111) corrections, HCO/COH ranges) were RECOMPUTED from the paper's own Table S5/S14 total energies and Table II before clearing — all reproduce within printing/stochastic precision. Bare abstains eliminated: every claim adjudicated, advised, routed, or reviewed-clean. Genuine method concerns (DMC time-step bias, two-cell finite-size extrapolation) routed as advisories, not manufactured.

routed_to_human.note: Abstract comparative on Pt(111) hybrid-cycle corrections. Recomputed from the paper's own delta_hyb (Table/Sec III.C) and delta_geom (Sec III.D): |delta_hyb(OOH)|=0.183 eV exceeds |delta_hyb(O)|=0.091 and |delta_hyb(OH)|=0.037 ('small for O/OH, larger for OOH'), and |delta_geom(OOH)|=0.041 < |delta_hyb(OOH)|=0.183 ('secondary correction'). Claim faithfully summarizes the body; no over-reach. No numeric verifier covers a magnitude-ordering predicate yet (synthesis candidate, DESIGN §8).
routed_to_human.quote:
For Pt(111), the resulting correction is small for O and
OH but larger for OOH, while the geometry-matched refinement gives only a secondary correction.

routed_to_human.note: Cu(111) opposite-sign claim. Recomputed: delta_hyb(HCO)=+0.042 eV, delta_hyb(COH)=-0.063 eV (Sec III.E / Table S14), signs genuinely opposite. Comparative claim faithful to the evidence; no over-reach. Numeric sign-comparison verifier not yet in the commons (synthesis candidate, DESIGN §8).
routed_to_human.quote: Applying the same cycle to HCO and COH on Cu(111) gives corrections of opposite sign

routed_to_human.note: Pt(111) Scheme-1 corrections delta_hyb(O)=-0.091, delta_hyb(OH)=-0.037, delta_hyb(OOH)=-0.183 eV. Cross-checked against Table II: Scheme1 - CHE adsorption energies reproduce exactly -0.090/-0.037/-0.183 eV for O/OH/OOH (within table printing precision). Internally consistent; no over-reach. A T0 formation-energy recompute verifier (E_form_HL - E_form_QMC from Table S5 total energies) is a synthesis candidate (DESIGN §8).
routed_to_human.quote:
δhyb(O) = −0.091 eV,
δhyb(OH) = −0.037 eV,
δhyb(OOH) = −0.183 eV.

routed_to_human.note: delta_geom(OOH)=-0.041 +/- 0.007 eV. Recomputed from Table S5 total energies as (E_frz - E_opt)_HL - (E_frz - E_opt)_QMC = 0.158 - 0.198 = -0.040 eV, rounding to the reported -0.041; the +/-0.007 matches the QMC relaxation-energy stochastic error. Faithful; no over-reach. T0 recompute over S5 is a synthesis candidate (DESIGN §8).

OOH relaxation energies: QMC 0.198 +/- 0.007 eV, CCSD(T)/CBS 0.158 eV. Recomputed from Table S5 (ev15) as E(OOHfrz) - E(OOHopt): SD-FNDMC = +0.198 eV, CCSD(T)/CBS = +0.158 eV — both reproduce to 3 decimals. Internally consistent; no over-reach. T0 recompute over S5 is a synthesis candidate (DESIGN §8).

routed_to_human.note: Cu(111) reference corrections delta_hyb(HCO)=+0.042, delta_hyb(COH)=-0.063 eV (the underlying numbers behind c2). Consistent with the Table S14 site data and the opposite-sign statement. No over-reach. T0 formation-energy recompute is a synthesis candidate (DESIGN §8).

δhyb(HCO) = +0.042 eV,
δhyb(COH) = −0.063 eV.

routed_to_human.note: Table II finite-size-extrapolated adsorption energies. Internal cross-consistency recomputed: for every adsorbate Scheme1(X) - CHE(X) reproduces delta_hyb(X) (O -0.090, OH -0.037/-0.036, OOH -0.183 eV) and Scheme2(OOH) - Scheme1(OOH) = 4.333 - 4.374 = -0.041 eV = delta_geom(OOH), all within table printing precision. Fully self-consistent; no over-reach. A T0 table-delta verifier over Table II is a synthesis candidate (DESIGN §8).

Table I comparative: |SD-FNDMC signed error for CHE-derived OOH| (+0.168 eV) >> |direct OOH formation error| (-0.025 eV). Recomputed from ev7: 0.168 > 0.025 holds; both errors equal dH_calc - dH_exp against the listed experimental references. Faithful comparison; no over-reach. T0 error-magnitude verifier is a synthesis candidate (DESIGN §8).

Reported OOH gas-phase vs surface-extracted geometry (O-O 1.35->1.43 A; H-O-O 105.0->102.1 deg). These are stated structural parameters, not a derived inference — nothing to over-claim; they correctly motivate the geometry-matched Scheme 2. No numeric verifier (geometry round-trip from coordinates) covers it yet (synthesis candidate, DESIGN §8).
routed_to_human.quote:
the O–O bond elongates from 1.35 ˚A in
the optimized gas-phase structure to 1.43 ˚A in
the surface-extracted geometry, while the O–H
bond length remains nearly unchanged and the
H–O–O angle decreases from 105.0◦to 102.1◦.

routed_to_human.note: Reported PBE-D3(BJ) fcc Pt lattice constant 3.93 A (16x16x16 k-mesh). A computed input parameter, plausibly close to the experimental ~3.92 A for Pt; descriptive, no over-reach. A T1 known-value-lookup verifier (lattice constant vs reference) is a synthesis candidate (DESIGN §8).
routed_to_human.quote:
The fcc Pt lattice constant optimized at this
level was 3.93 ˚A using a 16 × 16 × 16 k-point
mesh.

routed_to_human.note: Reported Pt(111) slab construction (3 layers, 12 Pt atoms, cell 5.55/5.55/20.54 A). Stated model setup; no derived claim to over-reach. A T0 geometry-consistency verifier (12 = 3 layers x 4 atoms; in-plane cell vs lattice constant) is a synthesis candidate (DESIGN §8).
routed_to_human.quote:
The Pt(111) slab was then constructed
as a three-layer slab containing 12 Pt atoms
in total, with cell dimensions of 5.55, 5.55,
and 20.54 ˚A.

Definitional, no over-reach. A T0 symbolic/equation verifier is a synthesis candidate (DESIGN §8).

routed_to_human.note: Reported DMC time step 0.005 a.u. with no time-step extrapolation. A stated sampling parameter; descriptive, no over-reach. (A method-appropriateness question about the fixed time step is routed separately as an advisory.)
routed_to_human.quote:
The final energies were obtained from fixednode diffusion Monte Carlo runs with a time
step of ∆τ = 0.005 a.u.

routed_to_human.note: Reported N^(-5/4) finite-size extrapolation between the 1x1 and 2x2 cells (Eq. 2). The exponent -5/4 = -1.25 and the two-point form are stated correctly. Definitional; no over-reach in the description. (Whether two cells suffice for this extrapolation is routed separately as a methodologist advisory.)
routed_to_human.quote:
Residual finite-size effects in adsorption energies were removed by N −5/4
extrapolation
[84] between the 1 × 1 and 2 × 2 surface cells

routed_to_human.note: Reported VASP convergence parameters (520 eV cutoff, 0.05 eV smearing, 1e-6/0.02 slab and 1e-8/0.002 bulk thresholds). Stated computational settings; nothing derived to over-reach. A T0 internal-consistency check (tighter bulk than slab thresholds) is a synthesis candidate (DESIGN §8).
routed_to_human.quote: a plane-wave cutoff of 520 eV, Gaussian smearing of 0.05 eV

routed_to_human.note: Reported Table I experimental reference values (H2O -2.506, OH 0.387, OOH 0.128, OH(CHE) 2.893, OOH(CHE) 5.140 eV) used as the denominator for signed errors. These are external reference inputs; their role is correctly described. A T1 known-value verifier (experimental thermochemistry lookup) is a synthesis candidate (DESIGN §8).

T4/T2 range-endpoint check, partially reconstructable. From Table S14 (ev16) the HCO Scheme2-Scheme1 endpoints reproduce the prose exactly: bridge -0.029 eV and top +0.046 eV (prose '-0.029 ... +0.045'). The COH min magnitude also matches (|top S2-S1| = 0.006 eV = prose lower bound). However the COH upper bound 0.032 eV falls at an fcc/hcp site NOT present in the extracted evidence (ev16 holds only bridge/top), so that one endpoint cannot be confirmed from the bound data. No over-reach detected in the reachable endpoints. Confirms-it: the full per-site Table S14 COH rows, then a T0 table-delta recompute.
routed_to_human.quote:
gies by only 0.006–0.032 eV depending on site,
whereas for HCO it ranges from −0.029 eV
at the bridge site to +0.045 eV at the top
site.

routed_to_human.note: T3 DMC time-step bias. A single fixed tau = 0.005 a.u. with NO time-step extrapolation leaves an uncontrolled O(tau) fixed-node DMC bias in the total energies. For all-electron-like pseudopotential DMC 0.005 a.u. is on the larger side; because the paper's corrections are differences of total energies (Scheme 1/2), much of the bias is expected to cancel, but the cancellation is assumed, not demonstrated. This is a method-appropriateness judgement, not an arithmetic error — LITMUS routes it rather than scoring it. Confirms-it: a tau -> 0 extrapolation (or two time steps) on a representative adsorbate showing the correction is stable to < its quoted stochastic error (~0.007 eV).
routed_to_human.quote:
The final energies were obtained from fixednode diffusion Monte Carlo runs with a time
step of ∆τ = 0.005 a.u.

routed_to_human.note: T3 finite-size extrapolation with two points. The N^(-5/4) form (Drummond et al.) is the standard leading single-particle finite-size correction, but fitting it from only the 1x1 and 2x2 cells gives an exactly-determined two-point extrapolation with no residual to assess curvature or higher-order/Coulomb (N^-1) terms. The functional form and exponent are stated correctly (the arithmetic is clean); whether two cells suffice to converge the adsorption-energy finite-size error is a methods judgement code cannot settle. Confirms-it: a third cell (e.g. 3x3) or an independent structure-factor/KZK correction agreeing with the two-point extrapolation within the reported ~0.02-0.04 eV uncertainties.
routed_to_human.quote:
Residual finite-size effects in adsorption energies were removed by N −5/4
extrapolation
[84] between the 1 × 1 and 2 × 2 surface cells

routed_to_human.note: Whether the SD-FNDMC hybrid-cycle correction scheme (Scheme 1/2) is an important or novel methodological advance for ORR adsorption energetics on Pt(111) — versus an incremental refinement of existing QMC-for-surfaces workflows — is a value judgement LITMUS does not score (DESIGN §3.5). The internal arithmetic is sound (see the claims-auditor clears); the worth of the contribution is routed to an editor / domain reviewer.
routed_to_human.quote:
For Pt(111), the resulting correction is small for O and
OH but larger for OOH, while the geometry-matched refinement gives only a secondary correction.
